cases.title: System automatically promotes top waitlist participant to Registered when seat is released
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen at capacity with waitlistEnabled true
Participant A has state Registered; participant B has state Waitlisted at FIFO position 1
Cancellation policy allows A to cancel before deadline
steps: Invoke registrationService.cancel for participant A's registration
Query registration state for A and B within the same committed transaction boundary
Query WaitlistEntry.promoted_at and waitlistPosition for B
Query Registered seat count for the event
cases.expected: A transitions to CancelledByUser; B transitions automatically from Waitlisted to Registered without manual organizer action; WaitlistEntry.promoted_at set and waitlistPosition cleared; Registered count remains at EventCapacity; promotion atomic with seat release per BR-08b and FR-12

cases.title: Testing-plan Flow B promotion segment keeps Registered count at capacity after auto-promotion
preconditions: Full event with waitlistEnabled true per testing-plan §6 Flow B setup
Participant A Registered and participant B Waitlisted at position 1
EventCapacity fixed at N with exactly N Registered before cancellation
steps: Release one seat by valid cancellation of participant A
Observe automatic promotion of participant B without separate promotion command
Count registrations in Registered state immediately after commit
cases.expected: Flow B cancellation → auto promotion segment completes; Registered count equals EventCapacity (never exceeds capacity per testing-plan §3 Capacity row and AC-04); promotion is system-triggered on seat availability per FR-12 and AC-02c

cases.title: promoteFromWaitlist transition Waitlisted to Registered when seat available via registration module
preconditions: Registration in Waitlisted state with active WaitlistEntry at position 1
registeredCount is less than EventCapacity (one seat available after seat-holder cancellation)
Registration window still open
steps: Release one seat via valid seat-holder cancellation on real database fixture
Observe promoteFromWaitlist transition executed by registration module in same transaction
Query registration state and status history for the waitlisted participant
cases.expected: Registration transitions Waitlisted → Registered per state machine; promoteFromWaitlist fires only when a seat is available; WaitlistEntry.promoted_at set and waitlistPosition cleared per FR-12 behavior

cases.title: FIFO promotion selects position 1 waitlist entry when one seat opens
preconditions: Event at capacity with three Waitlisted participants at positions 1, 2, and 3
One Registered participant eligible for valid cancellation
steps: Cancel the Registered participant to release exactly one seat
Read registration states for all three Waitlisted participants
Read waitlist entry positions and promoted_at for each entry
cases.expected: Only position-1 participant transitions to Registered with promoted_at set; positions 2 and 3 remain Waitlisted with unchanged relative order; BR-06a FIFO honored

cases.title: Flow B auto-promotion step after cancellation promotes waitlisted participant via API
preconditions: Full event with waitlistEnabled true per Flow B setup
Participant A Registered and participant B Waitlisted at position 1
A authenticated with valid JWT; cancellation allowed before deadline
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel as participant A
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status as participant B
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered as organizer scope
cases.expected: Cancel returns HTTP 200 with state CancelledByUser for A; cancel response may include optional promoted registration snapshot for B when promotion ran (BR-08b); B's registration-status returns state Registered with waitlistPosition null and updatedAt; organizer registrations list includes B as Registered; Registered count never exceeds capacity per AC-04; completes Flow B promotion segment

cases.title: GET registration-status returns HTTP 200 with Registered after automatic waitlist promotion
preconditions: Event at capacity with waitlistEnabled true
Participant A Registered; participant B Waitlisted position 1
A has cancelled successfully releasing one seat; B holds valid JWT
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel as participant A with Idempotency-Key
Inspect cancel POST response body for optional promoted registration payload
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status as participant B with Authorization bearer
cases.expected: Cancel POST returns HTTP 200 with state CancelledByUser; response may include optional promoted object with state Registered for B (BR-08b); subsequent GET registration-status returns HTTP 200 with body containing state Registered, registrationId, eventId, participantId matching B, waitlistPosition null, reasonCode null, and updatedAt after promotion timestamp; no manual promotion endpoint invoked

cases.title: Organizer-initiated cancellation triggers same automatic waitlist promotion
preconditions: Event at capacity with waitlistEnabled true
Participant A Registered; participant B Waitlisted position 1
Organizer Admin actor with event scope
steps: Invoke registrationService.cancelByOrganizer for A's registration with organizer actor context
Query registration states for A and B
cases.expected: A transitions to CancelledByOrganizer; B automatically promoted to Registered with promoted_at set and waitlistPosition cleared; seat release from organizer cancellation triggers the same promotion flow as participant cancellation per BR-08b

cases.title: Waitlisted to Registered promotion recorded in audit and status history when promotion succeeds
preconditions: Event at capacity with one Waitlisted participant at position 1
One Registered participant whose cancellation is permitted
steps: Cancel Registered participant to trigger promoteFromWaitlist transition
Query audit log for registration.promoted_from_waitlist action
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?registrationId={promotedRegistrationId} or query status_history store directly
cases.expected: Audit log contains registration.promoted_from_waitlist; status history contains transition from Waitlisted to Registered with promoteFromWaitlist trigger context per FR-12; promotion traceable per AC-12

cases.title: Sequential seat releases promote waitlist entries in FIFO order
preconditions: Event capacity 2 with 2 Registered and 2 Waitlisted at positions 1 and 2
Both Registered participants eligible for cancellation
steps: Cancel first Registered participant and verify promotion of position-1 waitlist entry
Cancel second Registered participant and verify promotion of former position-2 waitlist entry
Confirm final Registered count equals capacity with both former waitlist participants Registered
cases.expected: First cancellation promotes position 1; second cancellation promotes next-in-queue (former position 2); order preserved across multiple promotion cycles; exactly one promotion per freed seat per FR-12

cases.title: Promoted and cancelling participants see updated statuses in UI per Flow 2
preconditions: Full event with Registered participant A and Waitlisted participant B
Both participants authenticated in separate browser sessions or sequential logins
A can cancel before deadline
steps: As A, cancel registration on event detail and confirm if prompted
As B, refresh event detail or My Registrations
As organizer, open waitlist queue panel for the event
cases.expected: A sees cancelled terminal status; B sees Registered (seat confirmed) with waitlistPosition cleared and without ambiguous waitlist wording; organizer waitlist panel reflects promotion transition per user Flow 2 step 5

cases.title: Concurrent promotion attempts succeed once or return WAITLIST_ORDER_CONFLICT without double promotion
preconditions: Event capacity 2 with two Registered and exactly one Waitlisted
Only one waitlist slot to fill on single seat release
Database uses row lock on waitlist ORDER BY position ASC per database design
steps: Issue two concurrent valid cancellation requests that each would release one seat
Await both transactions to complete
Count Registered rows and verify waitlist entry promotion state
cases.expected: Exactly one waitlist participant promoted to Registered per freed seat; losing concurrent promotion attempts return WAITLIST_ORDER_CONFLICT (retry-safe per BR-06b) without corrupting queue order; Registered count never exceeds capacity (AC-02g, AC-04); second promotion promotes next queue entry only when a second seat was actually freed

cases.title: Waitlisted participant denied canceling another participant's registration to trigger promotion
preconditions: Event at capacity with participant A Registered and participant B Waitlisted
Participant C authenticated with valid JWT but no registration for the event
A's registrationId is known
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel as participant C using A's registrationId
Verify registration states for A and B unchanged
cases.expected: POST returns HTTP 403 unauthorized; A remains Registered; B remains Waitlisted; participant cannot operate on another's registration per FR-26; promotion cannot be indirectly forced by unauthorized cancel

cases.title: Promotion rejected when no seat is available at EventCapacity
preconditions: Registration in Waitlisted state
registeredCount equals EventCapacity (no free seat)
Active WaitlistEntry exists for the registration
steps: Attempt internal promoteFromWaitlist path without a preceding seat release or capacity increase via registration module
Verify registration state and Registered count unchanged
cases.expected: Promotion rejected with 409 Conflict or domain error INVALID_TRANSITION; registration remains Waitlisted; BR-03 capacity invariant preserved; promotion cannot occur without an available seat per FR-12 preconditions

cases.title: No promotion attempted when waitlist is empty on seat release
preconditions: Event at capacity with waitlistEnabled true
Zero Waitlisted registrations (full event, no queue)
One Registered participant eligible for cancellation
steps: Cancel the sole Registered participant
Query waitlist entries and Registered count
cases.expected: Cancellation succeeds with seat released (Registered count decreases by one); no erroneous promotion or phantom Registered rows created; system handles empty waitlist gracefully without error

cases.title: Waitlisted registration does not promote when registration window closes before seat opens
preconditions: Event at capacity with Waitlisted participant at position 1
Fixed clock advances past registration close while seat remains full
No seat release occurs before window close
steps: Trigger registrationClosed transition for Waitlisted participant per state machine
Attempt seat release after window closed without reopening registration
cases.expected: Waitlisted participant transitions to Expired on registrationClosed; promoteFromWaitlist does not apply after terminal Expired state; invalid transition returns 409 if promotion attempted on Expired registration

cases.title: Cancellation and waitlist promotion execute atomically in one transaction
preconditions: Event at capacity with one Waitlisted at position 1
Registered participant eligible for cancellation
Integration test harness with transaction visibility or rollback assertion
steps: Simulate cancellation mid-transaction failure after seat release but before promotion commit (or use transactional test fixture)
Verify database state on rollback versus successful commit path
cases.expected: On successful commit both cancellation and promotion persist together; on failure neither partial state remains — no freed seat without corresponding promotion when waitlist exists; satisfies testing plan §5 integration target for registration + waitlist promotion atomicity

cases.title: Registered count never exceeds capacity immediately after auto-promotion
preconditions: Event at capacity with multiple Waitlisted participants
Controlled cancellation releases one seat
steps: Execute cancellation triggering promotion
Query count of registrations in Registered state in same transaction snapshot
Repeat under light concurrent load with multiple waitlisted entries
cases.expected: registeredCount <= EventCapacity invariant holds immediately after promotion; testing-plan §3 Capacity under heavy concurrent requests invariant satisfied for promotion path; AC-04 and BR-03 never violated during auto-promotion

cases.title: Promoted participant receives understandable and consistent Registered status messaging
preconditions: Participant B promoted from Waitlisted to Registered after A cancels
B views event detail and My Registrations
steps: Observe status label and helper text on event detail for participant B
Compare status presentation with directly Registered participant on another event
cases.expected: Promoted participant sees plain-language Registered status (not ambiguous waitlist wording); label consistent with NFR-12 and design system status text; no conflicting duplicate labels for the same state

cases.title: Capacity increase triggers FIFO waitlist promotion for each newly available seat
preconditions: Event at capacity with waitlistEnabled true and at least two Waitlisted participants at positions 1 and 2
Organizer Admin authorized to increase EventCapacity per BR-22 with audit reason
steps: Record beforeState: Registered count equals EventCapacity, Waitlisted count >= 2
Increase EventCapacity by K available seats via EventRuleConfig update
Query registration states, WaitlistEntry.promoted_at, and Registered count after commit
cases.expected: System promotes exactly min(K, waitlistedCount) participants FIFO starting at lowest position; each promoted registration transitions Waitlisted → Registered with promoted_at set and waitlistPosition cleared; Registered count never exceeds new EventCapacity; promotion runs in same transaction as capacity update per FR-12 capacity-increase trigger
